_comment: GDAP relationship specification for the Sentinel to Defender XDR 1-Week Migration engagement. Implements Path A's GDAP half (and the only delegation mechanism for the Microsoft 365 / Defender XDR surface). Maps to the Partner Center create-GDAP-relationship workflow at partner.microsoft.com/dashboard/customers (Customers > [Customer name] > Granular delegated admin privileges > New request).
displayName: Simplicity IT - Sentinel to Defender XDR Migration
_duration_comment: ISO 8601 duration. 14 days covers a 7-day engagement plus a 7-day grace window for Day 7 follow-ups. Maximum permitted by Microsoft is 730 days; we intentionally use the shortest defensible window for security hygiene.
_autoExtendDuration_comment: Disabled. The relationship is one-shot; it does not auto-renew. Day 7 access teardown is a deliverable.

unifiedRoles._why: Day 6 cutover: 'Connect workspace' click in the Defender portal; analytics rule enable/disable; automation rule edits in the unified portal. Day 7 access teardown validation.
Day 1 read-only assessment of the Defender XDR surface; Day 6 parity validation in the unified portal; Day 7 handover walkthrough.
Day 5 Logic Apps playbook port: re-authorize external SOAR connectors (ServiceNow, Jira, Teams) in the unified portal.

_jit_note: Customers with Microsoft Entra ID P2 should configure these roles as JIT-eligible at the time of accepting the GDAP relationship. JIT-eligible users must request access via the My Access portal, and a customer-side approver (in a JIT approver security group) reviews each request before role activation. This matches the privileged-access model regulated buyers expect. Reference: learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/lighthouse/m365-lighthouse-setup-gdap
